stories.headline: Iran lawmakers present Hormuz management bill as US war heats up
stories.summary: Hardline MPs table plan that aims to formalise control over the strait and undermine any negotiations.

stories.headline: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
stories.summary: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".

stories.headline: US strikes Bushehr as Iran's parliament tables bill to control Hormuz
stories.summary: Four points in the port city hosting Iran's only nuclear power plant were hit by US projectiles on Tuesday, as Iran's parliament formally submitted a bill asserting Tehran's permanent control over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Sudan sentences RSF chief Hemedti to death: Who’s he, what’s he accused of?
stories.summary: Port Sudan trial of paramilitary leaders centres on atrocities in West Darfur.

stories.headline: Argentina war vets urge focus on football for England World Cup semifinal
stories.summary: One of sport's great rivalries sees Argentina face England in an ever feisty encounter in the 2026 edition's semifinal.

stories.headline: SoftBank’s Son ridicules AI critics for ‘spitting upwards’
stories.summary: Business leaders unwilling to embrace technology ‘should play the role of spouse’, says controversial investor

stories.headline: Zelenskyy joins Macron and European leaders in Paris for Bastille Day
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy joins Emmanuel Macron and European leaders at a Ukraine-focused Bastille Day parade in Paris showcasing military strength.

stories.headline: France deploys 70,000 security personnel across country for Bastille Day, World Cup semifinal events – Europe live
stories.summary: ‘No unruly behaviour will be tolerated’ after the match, according to France’s interior ministerHere is the first big moment of the day as the Garde républicaine plays the French national anthem, “La Marseillaise”.Macron is not singing, but Lecornu behind him – very much is. stories.headline: WHO warns DR Congo Ebola outbreak may be double the official tally
stories.summary: UN health agency says cases are underreported while doctors at outbreak epicentre threaten to go on strike.

stories.headline: What is Iran’s Pickaxe Mountain, the mystery site Trump warns he’ll attack?
stories.summary: Tensions escalate as the US president threatens to destroy Iran's fortified nuclear site in the south of Tehran.

stories.headline: Government reshuffle in Ukraine: Parliament dismisses Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko
stories.summary: After Ukraine's parliament accepted the prime minister's resignation, the composition of the next wartime cabinet remains unclear. It is not yet known which ministers will retain their posts across key portfolios.

stories.headline: EU bans gold imports from Sudan to curb money financing the war
stories.summary: The ban also restricts exports of mercury and cyanide to Sudan, chemicals widely used in gold mining.

stories.headline: How US commerce secretary's Epstein links were uncovered by British whistleblower
stories.summary: Simon Andriesz made the discovery about Howard Lutnick in publicly released Epstein files.

stories.headline: UAE condemns Iran's 'brazen' attack on tankers as US launches fresh strikes
stories.summary: The third consecutive night of US strikes on Iran came as Trump announced a 20% charge as part of a new blockade on the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Maine ICE Shooting: What We Know About the Death of Joan Sebastian Guerrero
stories.summary: A federal immigration agent shot and killed a man in a car on Monday morning in Biddeford, Maine. It was the second fatal encounter in a week involving an agent and a person in a vehicle.

stories.headline: Trump Says Fighting With Iran Has Resumed as He Orders Blockade and Tolls
stories.summary: After several days of strikes, the president notified Congress that fighting had begun again and announced shipping fees that his administration previously deemed illegal.

stories.headline: ICE Shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo Through Open Passenger Window in Houston, Witnesses Say
stories.summary: A congresswoman and a lawyer relayed witness accounts that diverge from Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s version of the fatal encounter.

stories.headline: SoftBank's Son says AI boom will require $5tn in annual investment
€2.8bn battle on the pitch: France and Spain face off in World Cup's priciest semi-final
stories.summary: Tuesday's clash between France and Spain in Texas is not just a footballing spectacle, it is also, by market value, the costliest semi-final the World Cup has staged, with the two squads worth a combined $3.2 billion (€2.8bn).

stories.headline: Singapore court orders Bloomberg to pay $356,000 to ministers in defamation case
stories.summary: The ministers claimed the piece, which cited their luxury bungalow deals, had tarnished their reputation.

stories.headline: Wall Street banks post blockbuster profits as equities trading booms
stories.summary: JPMorgan, Goldman, Citi and BofA kick off earnings season with strong results

stories.headline: RIP the US state department
stories.summary: America’s diplomats used to have global sway. Now they are besieged by their own president

stories.headline: Democrats are not in great shape
stories.summary: A fractious opposition is doing everything it can to spurn an open goal

stories.headline: Buffett drops Gates Foundation from $6bn Berkshire donation
stories.summary: Billionaire chair of conglomerate says he plans to leave holdings in company to four family foundations

stories.headline: Treasuries Rally as Cool CPI Data Cuts July Fed Hike Bets to 20%
stories.summary: US Treasuries rallied and traders pulled back from bets on near-term Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes after consumer prices data came in lower than forecast.

stories.headline: US CPI Falls for the First Time Since 2020
stories.summary: The US consumer price index fell 0.4% in June, dragged down by the biggest decline in gasoline prices since 2022, taking some pressure off the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates. Tiffany Wilding, economist at Pimco, examines the data on “Bloomberg Surveillance.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Barito Makes More Than $5 Billion Takeover Bid for EDC
stories.summary: Indonesia’s PT Barito Renewables Energy has made an offer to buy geothermal company Energy Development Corp. to expand in the Philippines, people familiar with the matter said.

stories.headline: Citi Stock Traders Post Record Revenue for Quarter
stories.summary: Citigroup says revenue from equities trading surged 45% to $2.3 billion in the second quarter from a year earlier. That's 11% higher than the record set in this year's opening months. Earnings per share came in at $3.15, beating all estimates. This is coverage from "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Vedanta Oil to Invest $5 Billion to Boost Output Fivefold
stories.summary: Vedanta Oil and Gas Ltd. will invest $5 billion to expand production by more than five times, underscoring India’s push to curb reliance on energy imports.

stories.headline: Dangote Ends Naira Pricing for Refined Oil Products in Nigeria
stories.summary: Dangote Refinery is halting the sale of petroleum products in naira within the Nigerian market, Punch Newspaper reported, citing failings in a landmark 2024 plan for the state-owned energy company to supply crude in local currency.

stories.headline: Goldman Breaks Own Stock-Trading Revenue Record Again
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs just keeps on breaking its own records on Wall Street. It posted $7.42 billion for a quarter with record-breaking stock-trading results, driven by financing and taking profit in arranging bets. Chris Marinac, Brean Capital director of research, breaks down the report on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank Says Cheap Yuan Hits Germany Harder Than Euro Area
stories.summary: China’s yuan is still undervalued versus the euro despite its recent gains, presenting a larger “competitiveness challenge” to Germany than to the wider currency union, according to Deutsche Bank AG.

stories.headline: Why Supreme Court Justices Are Asking for More Security
stories.summary: Supreme Court justices are asking lawmakers on Capitol Hill to increase their 2027 budget, with most of the additional funding earmarked for security. Ann E. Marimow, a New York Times reporter, explains why the justices say these measures are necessary to protect them from rising threats.

stories.headline: The court’s blockbuster term might prompt questions from lawmakers that go beyond the budget.
stories.summary: Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett, the first justices to appear before lawmakers since 2019, are requesting millions of dollars to address security concerns amid rising threats.

stories.headline: ‘I Am Frustrated’: Emails Show Blanche’s Role in Trump’s Retribution Campaign
stories.summary: The cooperation of Todd Blanche will be a flashpoint in his confirmation hearing to be attorney general on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Graham’s Death Complicates G.O.P. Agenda in Congress
stories.summary: Senators returned to the Capitol mourning their colleague, who played pivotal roles on multiple issues confronting lawmakers.

stories.summary: Issue must be addressed with ‘even greater rigour’, says government spokesman, after New York Times report on how it has become a spy hub for Vladimir PutinJapan has said it recognised the need to counter foreign intelligence better after the New York Times reported that Russia had turned the country into a “den of spies” and key source of weapons components.The newspaper, in an investigation published on Sunday, reported that thanks to “weak esp

stories.summary: At least 13 people, including five believed to be Britons, were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Spain's Almeria province.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s Death Transforms the Race for His Democratic Challenger
stories.summary: Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, has offered condolences to those who loved Mr. Graham while considering the political fallout from his death. Republicans are scrambling to arrange a new primary election.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s sister picked to serve the rest of his Senate term
stories.summary: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) announced his selection of Darline Graham Nordone to serve into January. She is expected to be sworn in Tuesday.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Unusually warm rivers affect French nuclear power plants
stories.summary: High temperatures and below average rainfall put pressure on waterways used to cool reactorsAbove average temperatures combined with below average rainfall across much of western and central Europe during June and the first half of July have placed increasing pressure on rivers, ecosystems and energy infrastructure. Persistent high pressure brought prolonged sunshine, suppressed rainfall and enhanced evaporation, causing river levels to fall and

stories.headline: German court convicts Iraqi couple of enslaving Yazidi girls
stories.summary: The pair, named only as Twana H.S. and Asia R. A., left Germany for Iraq in 2015 and became members of the Islamic State group.

stories.headline: Russian anti-war politician Boris Nadezhdin detained by police
stories.summary: Boris Nadezhdin attempted to run against Vladimir Putin at the 2024 presidential election, but was prevented from doing so.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade fire as tensions rise over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Within hours of the latest US attacks, Iran said it had struck US military assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain and Oman.

stories.headline: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office
stories.summary: Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years.

stories.headline: Volkswagen planning to cut up to 100,000 jobs globally
stories.summary: The group, which includes Porsche and Audi, has faced a steep fall in profits and tough competition from China.

stories.headline: UK-Switzerland deal to scrap roaming charges and allow Britons to use e-gates
stories.summary: The UK signs a new services deal with Switzerland it says will increase exports by £5.2bn annually "in the long run".

stories.summary: The interior minister has said the fire near Paris could have been set deliberately and two people have been arrested.

stories.summary: The world’s most formidable marketing machine faces growing threats

stories.headline: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
stories.summary: Iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.

stories.headline: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
stories.summary: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.

stories.headline: China evacuates nearly two million people as powerful typhoon makes landfall
stories.summary: Schools, work and outdoor activities have been suspended in Zhejiang province and many transport services cancelled.

stories.headline: Iran must pledge to stop shooting at ships in Strait of Hormuz, US officials say
stories.summary: US media say Iran has told US officials an attack on ships crossing the strait early this week was "a mistake".

stories.headline: Small plane crash in Bahamas kills 10 people
stories.summary: The country's prime minister called the crash, on the country's independence day, a tragedy.

stories.headline: Watch: Typhoon Bavi forecast to bring heavy rains and floods to Taiwan, Japan and China
stories.summary: See how the powerful storm is expected to move across east Asia on Saturday.

stories.headline: Palestinians mourn Gaza World Cup screenings organiser killed in Israeli strike
stories.summary: Aid worker Mohammed al-Wahidi had become a prominent humanitarian figure during the Israel-Hamas war.

stories.headline: Motherhood not all warm and fuzzy: Bollywood actor's play spotlights messier side
stories.summary: As a society, we take mothers for granted and raising children is a thankless job, says actor Kalki Koechlin.

stories.headline: Big fall in oil, gas and cargo ships taking US-backed Hormuz route after new strikes
stories.summary: Data shows a decline in the number of ships - many carrying oil and gas - going through the waterway after attacks this week.

stories.headline: Trump threatens more strikes on Iran as Tehran warns of 'fearless' response
stories.summary: Speaking at a Nato summit, the president says the US will "probably" strike Iran again but added that "anything that happens will be over quickly".

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US military says it hit missile sites and command centres while Iran says it attacked US bases.

stories.headline: Are Argentina being treated favourably at World Cup?
stories.summary: Egypt have alleged bias in favour of Argentina and favouritism towards their star player, Lionel Messi. Is this fair?
